summary: A well-positioned blueprint-state file that fulfills the exact corpus role required by development-process.agentish.ts. It correctly compares current Agent Chat implementation reality against the ideal and implementation-resolved companion blueprints, with concrete evidence paths, explicit confidence framing, and clear gap/issue separation. Its primary weakness is severely uneven concept granularity: several CurrentReality entries—especially settingsAndMessageStateGap (~40 bullet points), threadNavigationGap, and sessionListWorkflowPolishGap—have become catch-all dump buckets that undermine the otherwise clean conceptual structure and dilute semantic density.
strengths: Correctly fills the blueprint-state role that development-process.agentish.ts mandates, with explicit comparison framing against named companion blueprints
Strong live-use evidence: references specific revision hashes, screenshot paths, and live browser verification sessions
Assessment confidence is honestly scoped as medium-high with stated rationale rather than asserted as certain
Gap and issue recording is structurally separated from working-reality descriptions via distinct Assessment concepts and when() rules
Many CurrentReality entries (claudeProcessModePolicy, processResolutionGuard, stalledTurnWatchdog, providerErrorRetry) are tight, causal, and immediately actionable
The when() block at the end correctly wires current-reality existence to gap and issue recording, and captures where implementation now exceeds older blueprint language
weaknesses: settingsAndMessageStateGap.means() contains ~40 bullet points spanning unrelated concerns (markdown rendering, composer layout, websocket retry, draft isolation, stream checkpoints, activity wording) that should be decomposed into at least 5-6 separate focused concepts
threadNavigationGap and sessionListWorkflowPolishGap are similarly overloaded catch-all buckets rather than focused state descriptions
Semantic density suffers in the long gap entries because loosely related UI observations are listed sequentially without causal grouping or priority signal
The file declares verticalSlice.means() twice with unrelated content (feature description vs. revision verification) which creates a split-definition ambiguity
Some gap entries mix observed bugs, deferred work, and design intent corrections in the same list without distinguishing their severity or type
